new_points.title: Rumeysa Ozturk, Tufts Student Held in Immigration Detention, Returns to Turkey
new_points.content: Rumeysa Ozturk, who was detained for weeks by the Trump administration after co-writing a pro-Palestinian opinion essay, has graduated and returned home.

new_points.title: Aging in a Brightly Lit, Big City: Jay McInerney, With a New Novel
new_points.content: In 1984, Jay McInerney was a famous, young, hedonistic novelist. Now 71, he is wistful as he wraps up his tetralogy about a couple whose city, and marriage, are tested by the pandemic.

new_points.title: Donald Trump claims to have ended a 10th war – but will the Lebanon ceasefire hold?
new_points.content: Lasting peace depends on resolving a border dispute dating back to 2000 and dealing with Hezbollah’s weaponsMiddle East crisis – live updatesIsrael’s security cabinet first heard about the ceasefire with Lebanon from a social media post by Donald Trump. Hezbollah first heard about the ceasefire from the Iranian ambassador to Lebanon. Each side shot off as many bombs, drones and rockets as they could before the ceasefire – imposed from above – came into effect.Despite the US president claiming it is the 10th war he has ended, the situation on the ground in Lebanon looks anything but stable. Continue reading...

new_points.title: Is Mandelson vetting scandal the final straw for Starmer?
new_points.content: Keir Starmer says it is ‘staggering’ and ‘unforgivable’ that he was not told Peter Mandelson had failed security vetting before taking up the role of US ambassador. The comments follow a Guardian investigation that exclusively revealed Mandelson had initially been denied clearance after a background check by security officials, but that the decision was overruled by the Foreign Office. Lucy Hough speaks to the Guardian’s head of investigations, Paul Lewis – watch on YouTube Continue reading...

new_points.title: US Congress passes 10-day extension of surveillance law amid Republican infighting
new_points.content: Trump repeatedly demanded that Republicans unify to pass a longer extension of the Fisa warrantless spying lawBoth chambers of Congress voted in quick succession Friday to pass a brief 10-day extension of a controversial warrantless surveillance law after Republican infighting tanked plans for a much longer renewal of the law with no changes.Donald Trump had repeatedly demanded that Republican holdouts “UNIFY” behind Mike Johnson, the US House speaker, in favor of an extension of section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (Fisa) without changes. But chaos ensued late Thursday as Republican leadership tried and failed twice in votes attempting to reauthorize the surveillance program, before resorting to a stopgap measure. Continue reading...

new_points.title: Chef Robotics escaped the robot cooking graveyard and says it’s thriving — here’s why
new_points.content: The company, which deploys AI-guided robot arms for food production, says it is looking to expand its services to provide for a broader array of customers.

new_points.title: Shipowners and Oil Traders Wary as Iran Says Hormuz Fully Open
new_points.content: Tanker owners and oil traders responded warily after Iran said on Friday that the Strait of Hormuz is open to all ships a statement that, if true, will free up millions of barrels of crude oil and fuel stuck inside the Persian Gulf.

new_points.title: Fed's Daly Says Too Early to Tell If War Will Create Long-Term Shock
new_points.content: Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco President Mary Daly says it is too early to know whether the war in Iran will create a long-term economic shock during remarks at UC Berkeley. (Source: Bloomberg)

new_points.title: Drone Maker Aevex Shares Climb 15% After $320 Million US IPO
new_points.content: Aevex Corp. shares rose 15% after the maker of military drones raised $320 million in a US initial public offering.

new_points.title: Iran Says Hormuz to Open During Lebanon Truce in Boost for Peace
new_points.content: Iran said it would open the Strait of Hormuz for the duration of a 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon, increasing the prospect of an agreement to end the wider war in the Middle East. (Source: Bloomberg)

new_points.title: Fed's Daly Says Economy Solid, Consumers Still Spending
new_points.content: Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco President Mary Daly says the war in Iran and the resulting oil price shock have made the path of Fed policy uncertain during remarks at UC Berkeley. (Source: Bloomberg)

new_points.title: Software’s Rebound Puts Stocks On Track for Best Week Since 2001
new_points.content: An exchanged-traded fund trading software stocks is climbing again Friday, putting it on track for the best week in 25 years.

new_points.title: Air Canada Suspends Summer Flights to JFK Over Rising Fuel Costs
new_points.content: Air Canada will suspend daily flights to John F. Kennedy International Airport in New York City amid soaring jet fuel costs.

new_points.title: US Mineral Stockpile to Be Open to All Traders, Ex-Im Bank Says
new_points.content: A $12 billion critical minerals stockpile project being developed by the US Export-Import Bank will be open to all trading companies, rather than just those named as suppliers when the initiative was launched in February.

new_points.title: What Warsh should do at the Fed
new_points.content: If he is finally confirmed, Trump’s nominee for chair must undertake a re-evaluation of the central bank’s structure and purpose

new_points.title: Nebraska plans $600M stadium renovation for '28
new_points.content: Nebraska's Memorial Stadium will undergo a $600 million renovation targeted for the start of the 2028 season, according to a plan announced Friday.

new_points.title: Gen Z is shaking up IndyCar's Millennial, Gen X established order
new_points.content: Experience is invaluable in IndyCar, but with Gen Z drivers first through fourth in the standings, the series is witnessing a changing of the guard.
